description: Dark-field transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images comparing κ-carbide precipitation in Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C lightweight advanced high-strength steels with varying silicon content. Subfigure (a) shows the steel containing 0.59% Si, while subfigure (b) shows the steel containing 1.56% Si, both subjected to aging treatment at 490°C for 63 hours. The images reveal bright contrast corresponding to nanoscale κ-carbide precipitates homogeneously distributed within the austenitic matrix. Visual comparison clearly demonstrates that increasing silicon content results in both larger precipitate dimensions and higher number density of κ-carbides, indicating accelerated precipitation kinetics and increased volume fraction. Selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns in the insets confirm the crystallographic structure and orientation relationship of the precipitates. The 10 nm scale bar establishes the nano-scale size regime of these microstructural features. These micrographs provide direct visual evidence supporting the mechanism that silicon additions enhance κ-carbide formation by increasing carbon activity in austenite, thereby contributing to the observed age-hardening response.
